zone_6b_notes: Plant slips (not seeds) after soil warms thoroughly — late May in Farmington. Sweet potatoes need 100–120 frost-free days and hot soil; Missouri summers are ideal. Plant slips horizontally 4 inches deep. Black plastic mulch boosts soil temperature and weed suppression significantly. Harvest before first hard frost (mid-October); cure at 85–90°F and 90% humidity for 10 days to convert starches to sugars — stores months. Beauregard and Covington are standard orange-flesh varieties suited to Missouri.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant slips late May when soil reaches 65°F

water: moderate — 1 inch/week; reduce in last 3–4 weeks before harvest for sweeter roots
